resources.string: Bluetooth permission is required for peer-to-peer messaging without internet.
Location permission is required to discover nearby devices via Bluetooth.
Notification permission is required to alert you of new messages.
Triple tap to clear all data

bitchat runs in the background to maintain mesh network connections with nearby devices. Battery optimization can interrupt these connections, causing messages to be delayed or missed.\n\nDisabling battery optimization ensures reliable peer-to-peer messaging.
Note: You can change this setting later in Android Settings > Apps > bitchat > Battery
Your device doesn\'t require battery optimization settings. bitchat will run normally.
Your device doesn\'t require battery optimization settings. bitchat will run normally.
bitchat can run reliably in the background

• Ensures reliable message delivery
• Maintains mesh network connectivity
• Allows background message relay
• Prevents connection drops
Keeps the Bluetooth mesh running in the background
decentralized mesh messaging with end-to-end encryption
Communicate directly via Bluetooth LE without internet or servers. Messages relay through nearby devices to extend range.
Connect with people in your area using geohash-based channels. Extend the mesh using public internet relays.
Private messages are encrypted. Channel messages are public.
add proof of work to geohash messages for spam deterrence.
route internet over tor for enhanced privacy.
Create local Dogecoin addresses, request DOGE, and connect to a matching local node.
Tip: Triple-click the app title to emergency delete all stored data including messages, keys, and settings.
keep mesh active when app is closed (foreground service)
Selected Dogecoin %1$s wallet. Addresses and node RPC must match this network.
This mode uses a separate %1$s key, address, WIF, and RPC config.
Mainnet DOGE has real value. Verify the address and matching node before receiving or sending.
Practice on testnet before using real funds. Switch to mainnet only after your WIF is backed up and your node settings are verified.
This request posts your receive address to everyone in this chat.
I understand this Dogecoin address will be public in this chat.
Confirm address sharing before posting this request.
This peer advertises a fixed receive address. Address rotation is future work.
Let mesh peers pay you by @nickname

Off by default. When on, your fixed %1$s receive address is broadcast to mesh peers so they can pay you. This is a reusable, identity-linked address — peers who already saved it keep it even after you turn this off.

Let connected peers with no node broadcast their already-signed %1$s transactions through yours. You hold no keys and custody nothing — you can only relay. Off by default; enabled separately per network.
I understand I will broadcast OTHER people\'s mainnet transactions through my node.

When a single peer says it broadcast your transaction, optionally check a public block explorer for the txid to upgrade the receipt from “claimed” to “confirmed.” Off by default: the check tells that explorer this device is interested in the transaction.
Mainnet defaults to Blockchair. The URL must contain {txid}; HTTPS only, except local addresses.
No reachable node of your own. A connected peer can broadcast this signed transaction for you.
A peer will see and broadcast this transaction.
Relayed by one peer\'s node — its claim only, not corroborated or confirmed on-chain by this device. Verify the txid in a block explorer before treating it as settled.
Relayed and corroborated by two or more peers\' nodes. Not independently confirmed on-chain by this device — verify the txid to treat it as fully settled.
Claimed — broadcast to the Dogecoin network via the built-in light client. Not yet confirmed in a block; verify the txid in a block explorer before treating it as settled.
Enter at least 0.01 DOGE with up to 8 decimal places.
Your home node is set up — it can take over while the light client catches up.
Using home node · light client still syncing (%1$d behind)
Enter a Dogecoin %1$s Core RPC URL reachable from this device before refreshing balance or sending.

Use a Dogecoin Core RPC endpoint reachable from this device. Phones need a LAN or VPN URL for your node. Cleartext HTTP is only allowed for local, private, or .local hosts; public hosts must use HTTPS.
Enter a valid Dogecoin RPC URL. Use HTTPS for public hosts, or HTTP only for local, private, or .local node addresses.
Leave blank for Dogecoin Core 1.14 single-wallet RPC. For newer multiwallet nodes, enter the wallet name here and keep the RPC URL on the node base endpoint.

If Dogecoin Core reports authentication failed, configure rpcuser/rpcpassword or rpcauth for the node and enter those credentials here. You can paste a user:password token into RPC user and the app will split it. Android cannot read host dogecoin-cli cookie auth.
How do I connect my node?
Node status has not been checked yet.
Dogecoin %1$s node, wallet, and relay ready. Blocks: %2$d, headers: %3$d, peers: %4$d.
Dogecoin %1$s pruned node, wallet, and relay ready. Blocks: %2$d, headers: %3$d, peers: %4$d. Historical rescans require an unpruned node; future receives and visible UTXOs can still be used.
Dogecoin %1$s node syncing. Blocks: %2$d, headers: %3$d, progress: %4$.2f%%. Wallet balance and sending are paused.
Dogecoin %1$s node is reachable, but wallet RPC is not ready: %2$s
Dogecoin Core wallet RPC is unavailable.
Dogecoin %1$s node and wallet are ready, but broadcast relay is not ready: %2$s
Dogecoin Core has no active broadcast peers.
Node policy precheck is available for signed transaction reviews.
Node policy precheck is not available on this Core version. Mainnet signed transactions require an extra acknowledgement and rely on final broadcast acceptance.
Node rescanblockchain RPC is not available on this Core version. Historical rescans can still use importaddress before the first balance refresh for this address.
Node is on %1$s. Select Dogecoin %2$s or use a matching node.
Check node status after editing RPC settings before refreshing balance or sending.
Node settings edited — nothing is saved or contacted until you tap Save.
Test checks the address above without saving it. Tap Save to actually use it.
Node status below reflects your saved settings, not the edits above.
Your node reports no coins for this address yet. It may still be catching up on this address.
This is an unverified public HTTPS address, so bitchat won\'t send RPC credentials to it. Use your own node on a private network, or its exact Tailscale address (https://<machine>.<tailnet>.ts.net).
Direct .onion node addresses aren\'t supported — contacting one outside Tor would leak the onion name. Publish the node privately instead (e.g. Tailscale Serve).
This isn\'t a usable Dogecoin node address. Use http(s) to a local or private host, or an exact Tailscale HTTPS address.
This wallet stores one encrypted private key per Dogecoin network on this device. Mainnet DOGE has real value; keep a WIF backup before funding this wallet.
Invalid Dogecoin WIF for the selected network.
No WIF backup is recorded for this key. Copy and store the WIF before funding or sending from this address.
No WIF backup recorded for this testnet key. Backing it up is optional on testnet, but recommended to practice the flow.
Refresh balance to import this address as watch-only and list UTXOs from your matching local node.
Last-known balance is shown while node settings are rechecked.
Max send after fee: %1$s DOGE. Estimated fee: %2$s DOGE.
No recent activity found for this imported address.
Recovering older mainnet funds on Dogecoin Core 1.14? Run Rescan before the first Refresh for this address. Refresh imports watch-only without historical scan.

This address is already imported in Dogecoin Core. On Core 1.14, historical rescan for older funds now requires a newer unpruned Core with rescanblockchain, or a fresh Core wallet where Rescan runs before Refresh.
Address import status could not be checked: %1$s

This Dogecoin %1$s node does not support rescanblockchain. Refresh will import this address watch-only without a historical scan. If this address might already have older funds, cancel and run Rescan first.

This Dogecoin %1$s node does not support rescanblockchain. Continuing will import this address watch-only without a historical scan. If this address might already have older funds, cancel and run Rescan first.
Historical rescan is unavailable on a pruned Dogecoin node. Use an unpruned node to recover older wallet history.
Leave blank for a full historical scan. Enter a known funding height to limit a long mainnet rescan.
Enter a non-negative whole block height.
Node policy: soft dust %1$s DOGE, hard dust %2$s DOGE, incremental fee %3$s DOGE/kB.
Unable to calculate maximum spendable DOGE.
Paste a Dogecoin address or dogecoin: URI. The app signs locally with the selected network key and broadcasts through your configured node.
On this Dogecoin Core 1.14 node, Review send and Use max will import this address watch-only without historical scan. If this address might already have older funds, run Rescan before sending.
This address is already imported in Dogecoin Core 1.14. Review send and Use max can only spend UTXOs visible to this wallet; use a newer unpruned Core or fresh wallet rescan to recover older funds.
Clipboard does not contain a valid Dogecoin address or payment request.
Scan a Dogecoin address or payment request
QR code is not a valid Dogecoin address or payment request.
Scan a Dogecoin WIF private-key QR code
Camera permission is needed to scan Dogecoin QR codes.
Enter a valid Dogecoin address for the selected network.
Enter a positive DOGE amount with up to 8 decimal places.
Enter at least %1$s DOGE so the transaction creates a standard Dogecoin output.
Enter a fee rate of at least %1$s DOGE/kB.
Copy and store this mainnet WIF before signing a transaction from this generated wallet.
Selected Dogecoin network changed. Review the transaction again before broadcasting.
Signed transaction review expired. Refresh balance and review the send again before broadcasting.
Acknowledge the mainnet warning before broadcasting this signed transaction.
Acknowledge the high fee warning before broadcasting this signed transaction.
Acknowledge the unavailable node policy precheck before exporting or broadcasting this mainnet signed transaction.
Recheck node status before exporting the signed raw transaction. The app must verify selected inputs are still spendable.
Signed transaction review is inconsistent. Refresh balance and review the send again.
Dogecoin node policy rejected the signed transaction. Refresh wallet balance and review the send again.

Broadcast on Dogecoin %1$s
Txid: %2$s
To: %3$s
Amount: %4$s DOGE
Fee: %5$s DOGE
Total debit: %6$s DOGE
Change: %7$s DOGE

Network: Dogecoin %1$s
To: %2$s
Send: %3$s DOGE
Fee: %4$s DOGE
Total debit: %5$s DOGE
Change: %6$s DOGE
Signed at: %1$s. Review again if inputs or node policy change.
Your node pre-checked this transaction — looks good.
Node policy check rejected the signed transaction.
This node did not precheck the signed transaction before review. Export or broadcast will rely on final acceptance by your configured node or another broadcaster.
I understand this mainnet transaction was not prechecked by node policy before review.
High fee warning: this transaction pays %1$s DOGE in fees to send %2$s DOGE.
I understand this signed transaction pays a high Dogecoin fee if broadcast or shared.
This spends real mainnet DOGE if the configured node accepts the transaction. A copied or shared signed raw transaction can also be broadcast elsewhere.
I understand this signed transaction can spend real mainnet DOGE if broadcast.
Configured Dogecoin node cannot relay broadcasts right now. Recheck node status; you can still copy the signed raw transaction and broadcast it elsewhere.
Raw transaction export is paused until the configured node wallet is ready. This lets bitchat recheck selected inputs before exposing a mainnet-signed transaction.
Unable to share signed raw Dogecoin transaction
Write this key down and keep it somewhere safe. Anyone with it can spend your coins, and it can\'t be recovered for you.
On mainnet this is real money. Save this key outside this app before you receive or spend DOGE.
I\'ve saved this key somewhere safe outside this app.
Only show this when no one (and no screen recorder) can see your screen.
This replaces the current local %1$s wallet key with the pasted WIF. The imported receive address will be:
This imports a key that can control real mainnet DOGE. Verify the address before replacing the local wallet.
I understand this mainnet WIF can spend real DOGE and will replace this device\'s selected wallet key.
This deletes the local %1$s private key from this device. Any DOGE sent to its address is lost unless you already backed up the WIF.

The configured %1$s node will rescan wallet history for this address. This can take a long time, especially on mainnet. On Dogecoin Core 1.14, this must run before the first balance refresh for this address because importaddress performs the historical scan only while importing. Keep the app open and wait for it to finish before sending.
choose an action for this message or user
choose an action for this user
block all messages from this user
chat with people near you using geohash channels. only a coarse geohash is shared, never exact gps. do not screenshot or share this screen to protect your privacy.
location permission denied. enable in settings to use location channels.
system location is off. enable it in Android settings to find nearby channels.
add short permanent notes to this location for other visitors to find.
notes rely on geo relays. check connection and try again.
Please wait while your location is being determined
Location permission is required for notes
be the first to add one for this spot.
add a note for this place
developer utilities for diagnostics and control
logs peer joins/leaves, connection direction, packet routing and relays
turn roles on/off and close all connections when disabled
camera permission is needed to scan qr codes
open a private chat to view fingerprints
you have verified this persons identity.
compare these fingerprints with %1$s using a secure channel.
bitchat does NOT track your location.\n\nLocation services are required for Bluetooth scanning and for the Geohash chat feature.

• Bluetooth device scanning
• Discovering nearby users on mesh network
• Geohash chat feature
• No tracking or location collection
Android recommends background location so bitchat can scan for nearby devices while the app is not open. This keeps the mesh alive after reboot.
When settings opens, choose "Allow all the time".
- scan for nearby devices while the app is closed\n- reconnect after reboot\n- keep the mesh running in the background
We NEVER collect or store your location. Your privacy is safe.

Location services are not available on this device. This is unusual as location services are standard on Android devices.\n\nbitchat needs location services for Bluetooth scanning to work properly (Android requirement). Without this, the app cannot discover nearby users.

• Discover nearby users
• Create mesh network connections
• Send and receive messages
• Work without internet or servers
This device doesn\'t support Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), which is required for bitchat to function.\n\nbitchat needs BLE to create mesh networks and communicate with nearby devices without internet.
bitchat needs to run in the background to maintain mesh connections. battery optimization can interrupt these connections.
• reliable message delivery\n• maintains mesh connectivity\n• prevents connection drops
This should only take a few seconds
• no tracking or data collection\n• Bluetooth mesh chats are fully offline\n• Geohash chats use the internet
bitchat does NOT track your location
pan and zoom to select a geohash
Required to discover bitchat users via Bluetooth
Allow bitchat to connect to nearby devices
Required by Android to discover nearby bitchat users via Bluetooth
bitchat needs this to scan for nearby devices
Enable Wi-Fi Aware to discover and connect to nearby bitchat users over Wi-Fi.
Allow bitchat to discover nearby Wi-Fi devices
Recommended to scan for nearby devices while the app is in the background
Receive notifications when you receive private messages
Allow bitchat to send you notifications
Disable battery optimization to ensure bitchat runs reliably in the background and maintains mesh network connections
Allow bitchat to run without battery restrictions
Critical permissions were denied. bitchat requires these permissions to function:
Please grant these permissions in Settings to use bitchat.
Some permissions were revoked. Please grant all permissions to continue.
Channel %1$s is password protected. Enter the password to join.
Tor not available in this build
Tap the add-person button above to add a family member, then chat privately.
Added from a group · not verified
Add %1$s to your contacts so you can message them privately, one-to-one. This name is what the group said — it isn\'t verified. To be sure it\'s really them, add them by scanning their code.
Add more family to this chat to make a private group. Everyone\'s messages stay encrypted.
No other family members to add yet.
Check your Wi-Fi or mobile data
Try turning on a stronger connection
This can take a moment — you can turn it off if it doesn\'t help
Turn on only if your messages won\'t connect
A simple wallet to send and receive Dogecoin
Show this to your family member,\nand have them tap “Scan”.
No camera? Copy your code and send it to them (text, email…). They add you by pasting it under “Scan”.
Your code isn\'t ready yet — try again in a moment.
Allow the camera to scan a family member\'s code.
This name is only on your device — family members won\'t see it.
Enter a short name (not empty, not “anon”).
You can change this later in settings.
A friendly, reliable messenger for keeping in touch with family. The technical settings stay out of the way.
The full bitchat — every setting, the mesh tools, and the Dogecoin wallet, all exposed.
Payment reported — not yet seen by your wallet.
This report is already shown once in this conversation.
Receipt sent as a claim. The recipient must verify it with their wallet.
Tap to check with your wallet
This report is for %1$s; your wallet is on %2$s.
Receipt is already sent or queued for this transaction.
This personal-node check is not routed over Tor.
